<li data-leveltext="o" data-font="Courier New" data-listid="2" aria-setsize="-1" data-aria-posinset="1" data-aria-level="2"><span data-contrast="auto">A fear that many parents have is that who their children spend time with will determine who their child is, and it’s true that peer groups influence who we become. However, the theory that being trans is based on peer pressure and popularity has </span><strong>no scientific evidence base</strong> <span data-contrast="auto">(see: What about Rapid Onset Gender Dysphoria?). </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":160,"335559740":259}"> </span></li>
<li data-leveltext="o" data-font="Courier New" data-listid="2" aria-setsize="-1" data-aria-posinset="2" data-aria-level="2"><span data-contrast="auto">Your child’s friends may have encouraged your child to question and explore their gender — this isn’t a bad thing! Regardless of the outcome of the exploration, </span><strong>questioning their gender will result in your child knowing themselves better.</strong><span data-contrast="auto"> It is a beneficial, lifelong skill to be able to question yourself and explore who you are.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":160,"335559740":259}"> </span></li>
</ul>

		</div>
	</div>
</div></div><div class="vc_tta-panel" id="1646177589504-a7633676-18b9" data-vc-content=".vc_tta-panel-body"><div class="vc_tta-panel-heading"><h4 class="vc_tta-panel-title vc_tta-controls-icon-position-left"><a href="#1646177589504-a7633676-18b9" data-vc-accordion data-vc-container=".vc_tta-container"><span class="vc_tta-title-text">My faith says it’s wrong to be transgender. What do I do?</span><i class="vc_tta-controls-icon vc_tta-controls-icon-plus"></i></a></h4></div><div class="vc_tta-panel-body">
	<div class="wpb_text_column wpb_content_element " >
		<div class="wpb_wrapper">
			<ul>
<li data-leveltext="o" data-font="Courier New" data-listid="2" aria-setsize="-1" data-aria-posinset="3" data-aria-level="2"><span data-contrast="auto">This is a complicated question that everyone will navigate differently. We can’t tell you how to navigate your personal relationship with spirituality and faith, nor how to navigate your relationship with any religious institutions you belong to. </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":160,"335559740":259}"> </span></li>
<li data-leveltext="o" data-font="Courier New" data-listid="2" aria-setsize="-1" data-aria-posinset="4" data-aria-level="2"><span data-contrast="auto">We encourage you to </span><strong>talk with your loved one</strong> <span data-contrast="auto">about how they feel about their faith institutions and what the teachings are. You might listen to their emotions about it and consider their opinions about the course to take. You might be able to explore options for next steps together, whether that be navigating your current faith tradition, advocating for change or changing faith traditions.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":160,"335559740":259}"> </span></li>
<li data-leveltext="o" data-font="Courier New" data-listid="2" aria-setsize="-1" data-aria-posinset="5" data-aria-level="2"><span data-contrast="auto">Some ideas that have been helpful to some people during this process include:</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":160,"335559740":259}"> </span></li>
</ul>
<ul>
<li style="list-style-type: none;">
<ul>
<li data-leveltext="" data-font="Wingdings" data-listid="2" aria-setsize="-1" data-aria-posinset="1" data-aria-level="3"><strong>Check in with your own belief system and connection to your higher power.</strong><span data-contrast="auto"> Some people struggle due to their internal, “gut” feeling about what is right being at odds with the religious teachings they follow. It can be helpful to reflect on some of the variety of perspectives you may uncover from prayer and meditation, from your own upbringing, from religious leadership and from your personal relationship with your higher power.</span></li>
<li data-leveltext="" data-font="Wingdings" data-listid="2" aria-setsize="-1" data-aria-posinset="1" data-aria-level="3"><strong>Access resources</strong><span data-contrast="auto"> from those who have come before you. There are groups of people, organizations and other supports for many major religions and groups within religions. There are also books written by parents and other family members navigating religion as their loved one comes out as transgender.</span></li>
<li data-leveltext="" data-font="Wingdings" data-listid="2" aria-setsize="-1" data-aria-posinset="1" data-aria-level="3"><span data-contrast="auto">Know that there are histories in your religion, which may include previous positions that have changed or been reinterpreted. It can be useful to </span><strong>notice that your religious teachings continue to evolve</strong><span data-contrast="auto"> over time, often in response to cultural context.</span></li>
<li data-leveltext="" data-font="Wingdings" data-listid="2" aria-setsize="-1" data-aria-posinset="1" data-aria-level="3"><span data-contrast="auto">You may consider </span><strong>talking to your faith leader and faith community</strong><span data-contrast="auto">, including advocating for change. We have heard and witnessed faith communities changing due to one of their community members coming out.</span></li>
<li data-leveltext="" data-font="Wingdings" data-listid="2" aria-setsize="-1" data-aria-posinset="1" data-aria-level="3"><span data-contrast="auto">There can be a great deal of loss in losing faith communities and structures due to being transgender or having a transgender loved on. You may be in a position to </span><strong>be your loved one’s spiritual guide or spiritual elder</strong><span data-contrast="auto"> and to help them navigate their own relationship with faith or their higher power in the context of being their authentic self.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":160,"335559740":259}"> </span></li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<ul>
<li style="list-style-type: none;"></li>
</ul>

		</div>
	</div>
</div></div><div class="vc_tta-panel" id="1646177621279-0d645042-8ffd" data-vc-content=".vc_tta-panel-body"><div class="vc_tta-panel-heading"><h4 class="vc_tta-panel-title vc_tta-controls-icon-position-left"><a href="#1646177621279-0d645042-8ffd" data-vc-accordion data-vc-container=".vc_tta-container"><span class="vc_tta-title-text">My loved one has significant trauma - shouldn't we wait to see if their gender identity changes back once the trauma is treated?</span><i class="vc_tta-controls-icon vc_tta-controls-icon-plus"></i></a></h4></div><div class="vc_tta-panel-body">
	<div class="wpb_text_column wpb_content_element " >
		<div class="wpb_wrapper">
			<ul>
<li data-leveltext="o" data-font="Courier New" data-listid="2" aria-setsize="-1" data-aria-posinset="1" data-aria-level="2"><span data-contrast="auto">We have </span><strong>no evidence that trauma causes transgender and/or nonbinary identity</strong><span data-contrast="auto">. </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":160,"335559740":259}"> </span></li>
</ul>
<ul>
<li style="list-style-type: none;">
<ul>
<li data-leveltext="" data-font="Wingdings" data-listid="2" aria-setsize="-1" data-aria-posinset="1" data-aria-level="3"><span data-contrast="auto">While transgender people have high rates of trauma, this has been linked to discrimination and many trans folks report questioning their gender prior to trauma occurring (</span><a href="https://transequality.org/sites/default/files/docs/usts/USTS-Full-Report-Dec17.pdf" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><span data-contrast="none">James et al., 2016</span></a><span data-contrast="auto">). </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":160,"335559740":259}"> </span></li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<ul>
<li style="list-style-type: none;">
<ul>
<li data-leveltext="" data-font="Wingdings" data-listid="2" aria-setsize="-1" data-aria-posinset="1" data-aria-level="3"><span data-contrast="auto">There are many people who experience trauma who are not transgender/nonbinary, and there are many people who are transgender/nonbinary who have not experienced trauma.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":160,"335559740":259}"> </span></li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>
<ul>
<li data-leveltext="o" data-font="Courier New" data-listid="2" aria-setsize="-1" data-aria-posinset="1" data-aria-level="2"><span data-contrast="auto">If trauma were to influence gender identity, it does not follow that trauma recovery would result in a change “back.” </span><strong>Trauma recovery does not return someone to who they were prior to trauma</strong><span data-contrast="auto">; it helps them move forward in their life, integrating the trauma as part of their story but not all of their story. </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":160,"335559740":259}"> </span></li>
<li data-leveltext="o" data-font="Courier New" data-listid="2" aria-setsize="-1" data-aria-posinset="2" data-aria-level="2"><span data-contrast="auto">If someone’s identity does change, that’s OK! We have </span><strong>no attachment to the outcome of someone’s gender journey</strong><span data-contrast="auto">. Whether or not your loved one identifies in a different way later, support of their identity exploration and their healing process will help them feel safe and supported.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":160,"335559740":259}"> </span></li>
<li data-leveltext="o" data-font="Courier New" data-listid="2" aria-setsize="-1" data-aria-posinset="3" data-aria-level="2"><span data-contrast="auto">A large part of the trauma recovery process is re-learning to trust yourself. To that end, an essential part of trauma therapy is for clinicians to </span><strong>believe patients and support patients in believing themselves</strong><b><span data-contrast="auto">, </span></b><span data-contrast="auto">including their ability to trust what they know about their identity, life decisions and interpersonal relationships. </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":160,"335559740":259}"> </span></li>
</ul>

		</div>
	</div>
</div></div><div class="vc_tta-panel" id="1646177663287-4689922d-13e1" data-vc-content=".vc_tta-panel-body"><div class="vc_tta-panel-heading"><h4 class="vc_tta-panel-title vc_tta-controls-icon-position-left"><a href="#1646177663287-4689922d-13e1" data-vc-accordion data-vc-container=".vc_tta-container"><span class="vc_tta-title-text">Isn't this just a political issue?</span><i class="vc_tta-controls-icon vc_tta-controls-icon-plus"></i></a></h4></div><div class="vc_tta-panel-body">
	<div class="wpb_text_column wpb_content_element " >
		<div class="wpb_wrapper">
			<ul>
<li data-leveltext="o" data-font="Courier New" data-listid="2" aria-setsize="-1" data-aria-posinset="1" data-aria-level="2"><strong>No, transgender and nonbinary identities have existed for all of human history</strong><span data-contrast="auto"> and the focus on trans and nonbinary issues in the political arena has come and gone several times over the history of the United States.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":160,"335559740":259}"> </span></li>
</ul>
<ul>
<li data-leveltext="o" data-font="Courier New" data-listid="2" aria-setsize="-1" data-aria-posinset="1" data-aria-level="2"><span data-contrast="auto">Some people welcome the discussion into the political sphere, viewing it as an opportunity to</span> <strong>increase access for transgender and nonbinary people to human rights and legal protection from discrimination</strong><span data-contrast="auto">. Others note that the increased focus has </span><strong>increased danger and bullying</strong><span data-contrast="auto">, with politicians using this as an opportunity to say harmful things about transgender people and present legislation that harms transgender and nonbinary people. </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":160,"335559740":259}"> </span></li>
<li data-leveltext="o" data-font="Courier New" data-listid="2" aria-setsize="-1" data-aria-posinset="2" data-aria-level="2"><span data-contrast="auto">It is difficult to have your identity be the center of a political discussion; it can feel dehumanizing. The political discussions tend to be about whether transgender and nonbinary people should have access to the rights and privileges that all participants in U.S. society have — access to public restrooms, access to sports teams and educational institutions, access to healthcare, etc. As you support your loved one in navigating this topic, </span><strong>consider discussing with them what it’s like for them</strong><span data-contrast="auto"> to see this legislation being proposed and passed. Note that they may find it too distressing to talk about. </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":160,"335559740":259}"> </span></li>
</ul>

		</div>
	</div>
</div></div><div class="vc_tta-panel" id="1646177711609-6e743a78-a006" data-vc-content=".vc_tta-panel-body"><div class="vc_tta-panel-heading"><h4 class="vc_tta-panel-title vc_tta-controls-icon-position-left"><a href="#1646177711609-6e743a78-a006" data-vc-accordion data-vc-container=".vc_tta-container"><span class="vc_tta-title-text">Why are you "affirming" their gender identity? We would prefer you to challenge it since we do not think it is authentic.</span><i class="vc_tta-controls-icon vc_tta-controls-icon-plus"></i></a></h4></div><div class="vc_tta-panel-body">
	<div class="wpb_text_column wpb_content_element " >
		<div class="wpb_wrapper">
			<ul>
<li data-leveltext="o" data-font="Courier New" data-listid="2" aria-setsize="-1" data-aria-posinset="1" data-aria-level="2"><strong>Challenging gender identity is not a recommended form of treatment and the evidence that we have suggests that it is harmful.</strong><span data-contrast="auto"> We use evidence-based treatments and always work to be up-to-date to ensure the best outcomes. </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":160,"335559740":259}"> </span></li>
</ul>
<ul>
<li style="list-style-type: none;">
<ul>
<li data-leveltext="" data-font="Wingdings" data-listid="2" aria-setsize="-1" data-aria-posinset="1" data-aria-level="3"><span data-contrast="auto">The American Psychological Association released </span><a href="https://www.apa.org/about/policy/resolution-gender-identity-change-efforts.pdf" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><span data-contrast="none">a resolution in Feb 2021</span></a><span data-contrast="auto"> that outlines the significant harm likely to result from attempts to pressure individuals to conform to the gender norms they were assigned at birth.</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":160,"335559740":259}"> </span></li>
<li data-leveltext="" data-font="Wingdings" data-listid="2" aria-setsize="-1" data-aria-posinset="2" data-aria-level="3"><span data-contrast="auto">There is also an abundance of evidence of the benefits of acceptance and support for transgender and nonbinary people from family, social transition, and, if desire, medical transition (</span><a href="https://psycnet.apa.org/doi/10.1111/fare.12304" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><span data-contrast="none">Catalpa & McGuire, 2018</span></a><span data-contrast="auto">; </span><a href="https://doi.org/10.1089/lgbt.2015.0111" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><span data-contrast="none">Klein & Golub, 2016</span></a><span data-contrast="auto">; </span><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jadohealth.2016.07.026" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><span data-contrast="none">McConnell et al. 2016</span></a><span data-contrast="auto">; </span><a href="https://doi.org/10.1146/annurev-clinpsy-021815-093153" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><span data-contrast="none">Russell & Fish, 2016</span></a><span data-contrast="auto">; </span><a href="https://doi.org/10.1111/j.1744-6171.2010.00246.x" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><span data-contrast="none">Ryan et al., 2010</span></a><span data-contrast="auto">; </span><a href="https://www.researchgate.net/publication/284551812_Impacts_of_Strong_Parental_Support_for_Trans_Youth" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><span data-contrast="none">Travers et al., 2012</span></a><span data-contrast="auto"> , </span><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jaac.2016.10.016" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><span data-contrast="none">Durwood, McLaughlin, and Olson, 2017</span></a><span data-contrast="auto">; </span><a href="https://doi.org/10.1080/00224499.2017.1415291" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><span data-contrast="none">Katz-Wise et al., 2018</span></a><span data-contrast="auto">; </span><a href="https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/fare.12226" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><span data-contrast="none">Kuvalanka et al., 2017</span></a><span data-contrast="auto">; </span><a href="https://doi.org/10.1542/peds.2015-3223" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><span data-contrast="none">Olson et al., 2016</span></a><span data-contrast="auto">; </span><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jadohealth.2013.07.019" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><span data-contrast="none">Simons et al., 2013</span></a><span data-contrast="auto">; </span><a href="https://doi.org/10.1089/trgh.2017.0031" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><span data-contrast="none">Veale et al., 2017, </span></a><a href="https://doi.org/10.3390/ijerph17249525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><span data-contrast="none">Sevelius et al., 2020,</span></a> <a href="https://doi.org/10.3109/09540261.2015.1089217" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><span data-contrast="none">Jones et al, 2016</span></a><span data-contrast="auto">; </span><a href="https://doi.org/10.1002/eat.23539" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><span data-contrast="none">Nowaskie et al., 2021</span></a><span data-contrast="auto">; </span><a href="https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jsxm.2018.01.017" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><span data-contrast="none">Owen-Smith et al., 2018</span></a><span data-contrast="auto">; </span><a href="https://doi.apa.org/doi/10.1037/hea0000497" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><span data-contrast="none">Testa et al., 2017, </span></a><a href="https://doi.org/10.1080/00918369.2012.653300" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><span data-contrast="none">de Vries et al., 2012</span></a><span data-contrast="auto">; </span><a href="https://doi.org/10.1002/erv.2576" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><span data-contrast="none">Jones et al., 2018</span></a><span data-contrast="auto">).</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":160,"335559740":259}"> </span></li>
<li data-leveltext="" data-font="Wingdings" data-listid="2" aria-setsize="-1" data-aria-posinset="3" data-aria-level="3"><span data-contrast="auto">The </span><a href="https://policysearch.ama-assn.org/policyfinder/detail/gender%20identity?uri=%2FAMADoc%2FHOD.xml-0-805.xml" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><span data-contrast="none">American Medical Association</span></a><span data-contrast="auto">, the </span><a href="https://publications.aap.org/pediatrics/article/142/4/e20182162/37381/Ensuring-Comprehensive-Care-and-Support-for" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><span data-contrast="none">American Academy of Pediatrics</span></a><span data-contrast="auto">, the </span><a href="https://store.samhsa.gov/product/Ending-Conversion-Therapy-Supporting-and-Affirming-LGBTQ-Youth/SMA15-4928" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><span data-contrast="none">Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration</span></a><span data-contrast="auto">, the </span><a href="https://www.aacap.org/AACAP/Policy_Statements/2018/Conversion_Therapy.aspx" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><span data-contrast="none">American Academy of Child and Adolescent Psychiatry</span></a><span data-contrast="auto"> and the </span><a href="https://www.psychiatry.org/newsroom/news-releases/apa-reiterates-strong-opposition-to-conversion-therapy" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><span data-contrast="none">American Psychiatric Association</span></a><span data-contrast="auto">, along with many other organizations, have put out statements opposing LGBT conversion therapy and supporting affirming therapy.</span></li>
</ul>
</li>
</ul>

		</div>
	</div>
</div></div><div class="vc_tta-panel" id="1646177728051-ddda498c-a16a" data-vc-content=".vc_tta-panel-body"><div class="vc_tta-panel-heading"><h4 class="vc_tta-panel-title vc_tta-controls-icon-position-left"><a href="#1646177728051-ddda498c-a16a" data-vc-accordion data-vc-container=".vc_tta-container"><span class="vc_tta-title-text">What resources would you recommend so that I can learn more?</span><i class="vc_tta-controls-icon vc_tta-controls-icon-plus"></i></a></h4></div><div class="vc_tta-panel-body">
	<div class="wpb_text_column wpb_content_element " >
		<div class="wpb_wrapper">
			<ul>
<li data-leveltext="o" data-font="Courier New" data-listid="2" aria-setsize="-1" data-aria-posinset="1" data-aria-level="2"><a href="https://pflag.org/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><span data-contrast="none">PFLAG</span></a><span data-contrast="none"> – Offers monthly support meetings for parents and loved ones; some chapters host gender-specific meetings in addition to general meetings </span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":160,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li data-leveltext="o" data-font="Courier New" data-listid="2" aria-setsize="-1" data-aria-posinset="2" data-aria-level="2"><a href="https://transfamilies.org/video-support-groups#1519765433322-73b23e35-4013" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><span data-contrast="none">Trans Families Online Support Groups</span></a><span data-contrast="none"> – Offers general groups and specific groups for parents of color, dads, Latinx and Spanish-speaking parents, parents of nonbinary youth, parents of children who are also neurodivergent (e.g., have autism, ADHD)</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":160,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li data-leveltext="o" data-font="Courier New" data-listid="2" aria-setsize="-1" data-aria-posinset="3" data-aria-level="2"><a href="https://bookstore.pflag.org/site/index.php?app=ecom&ns=prodshow&ref=PFLAG-007" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><span data-contrast="none">PFLAG’s Our Trans Loved One</span></a><span data-contrast="none"> – Offers information, first-person stories and expert guidance to those who have a loved one who comes out as trans, nonbinary or otherwise gender expansive</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":160,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li data-leveltext="o" data-font="Courier New" data-listid="2" aria-setsize="-1" data-aria-posinset="4" data-aria-level="2"><a href="https://raisingchildren.net.au/pre-teens/development/pre-teens-gender-diversity-and-gender-dysphoria/gender-identity,-diversity-and-dysphoria-supporting-your-child" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><span data-contrast="none">Gender identity, diversity and dysphoria: supporting your child</span></a><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":160,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li data-leveltext="o" data-font="Courier New" data-listid="2" aria-setsize="-1" data-aria-posinset="5" data-aria-level="2"><a href="https://genderspectrum.org/audiences/parents-and-family" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><span data-contrast="none">Gender Spectrum’s Parents & Family Guides</span></a><span data-contrast="none"> and</span><a href="https://www.genderspectrum.org/articles/schools-in-transition" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><span data-contrast="none"> School Guide</span></a><span data-contrast="none"> and </span><a href="https://www.tfaforms.com/4721601" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><span data-contrast="none">Support Groups for Parents of Gender Diverse Children & Teens </span></a><span data-contrast="none"> (</span><span data-contrast="none">Parent/Caregiver Support Group, Fathers/Masculine-of-Center Support Group, Grandparent Support Group, Parent of Nonbinary Child/Teen Support Group, Black/Indigenous/People of Color (BIPOC) Support Group)</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":160,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li data-leveltext="o" data-font="Courier New" data-listid="2" aria-setsize="-1" data-aria-posinset="6" data-aria-level="2"><a href="https://pediatrics.aappublications.org/content/142/4/e20182162" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><span data-contrast="none">American Association of Pediatrics: Ensuring Comprehensive Care and Support for Transgender and Gender-Diverse Children and Adolescents</span></a><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":160,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
</ul>
<ul>
<li data-leveltext="o" data-font="Courier New" data-listid="2" aria-setsize="-1" data-aria-posinset="1" data-aria-level="2"><span data-contrast="auto">Here are some </span><strong>books</strong> <span data-contrast="auto">we like for family members:</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":160,"335559740":259}"> </span></li>
</ul>
<ul>
<li data-leveltext="" data-font="Wingdings" data-listid="2" aria-setsize="-1" data-aria-posinset="1" data-aria-level="3"><a href="https://www.amazon.com/Transgender-Teen-Professionals-Supporting-Non-Binary/dp/1627781749/ref=sr_1_1?dchild=1&keywords=transgender+teen&qid=1629859351&s=books&sr=1-1" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><span data-contrast="none">Trangender Teen: A Handbook for Parents and Professionals Supporting Transgender and Non-Binary Teens</span></a><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":160,"335559740":259}"> </span></li>
<li data-leveltext="" data-font="Wingdings" data-listid="2" aria-setsize="-1" data-aria-posinset="2" data-aria-level="3"><a href="https://www.amazon.com/Raising-Transgender-Child-Complete-Caregivers/dp/1580056350" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><span data-contrast="none">Raising the Transgender Child: A Complete Guide for Parents, Families, & Caregivers, Dr. Michele Angello & Alisa Bowman, 2016</span></a><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":160,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li data-leveltext="" data-font="Wingdings" data-listid="2" aria-setsize="-1" data-aria-posinset="3" data-aria-level="3"><a href="https://www.amazon.com/Life-Isnt-Binary-Beyond-Between/dp/1785924796/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><span data-contrast="none">Life Isn’t Binary</span></a><span data-contrast="auto"> by Iantaffi and Barker about the nonbinary identities, 2020</span><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":160,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>
<li data-leveltext="" data-font="Wingdings" data-listid="2" aria-setsize="-1" data-aria-posinset="4" data-aria-level="3"><a href="https://www.amazon.com/Becoming-Ally-Gender-Expansive-Child-Parents/dp/1785920510" target="_blank" rel="noopener"><span data-contrast="none">Becoming an Ally to the Gender-Expansive Child : A Guide for Parents and Carers, Anna Bianchi, 2017</span></a><span data-ccp-props="{"201341983":0,"335559739":160,"335559740":240}"> </span></li>

        document.cookie = name + "=" + value + expires + ";path=/";
    }
 
    function getParam(p) {
        var match = RegExp('[?&]' + p + '=([^&]*)').exec(window.location.search);
        return match && decodeURIComponent(match[1].replace(/\+/g, ' '));
    }
    var gclid = getParam('gclid');
    if (gclid) {
        var gclsrc = getParam('gclsrc');
        if (!gclsrc || gclsrc.indexOf('aw') !== -1) {
            setCookie('gclid', gclid, 90);
        }
    }
 
    function readCookie(name) {
        var n = name + "=";
        var cookie = document.cookie.split(';');
        for (var i = 0; i < cookie.length; i++) {
            var c = cookie[i];
            while (c.charAt(0) == ' ') {
                c = c.substring(1, c.length);
            }
            if (c.indexOf(n) == 0) {
                return c.substring(n.length, c.length);
            }
        }
        return null;
    }
 
    window.onload = function() {
        document.getElementById('gclid').value = readCookie('gclid');
    }
</script>

	<script type='text/javascript'>( $ => {
	/**
	 * Displays toast message from storage, it is used when the user is redirected after login
	 */
	if ( window.sessionStorage ) {
		$( window ).on( 'tcb_after_dom_ready', () => {
			const message = sessionStorage.getItem( 'tcb_toast_message' );

			if ( message ) {
				tcbToast( sessionStorage.getItem( 'tcb_toast_message' ), false );
				sessionStorage.removeItem( 'tcb_toast_message' );
			}
		} );
	}

	/**
	 * Displays toast message
	 *
	 * @param {string}   message  - message to display
	 * @param {Boolean}  error    - whether the message is an error or not
	 * @param {Function} callback - callback function to be called after the message is closed
	 */
	function tcbToast( message, error, callback ) {
		/* Also allow "message" objects */
		if ( typeof message !== 'string' ) {
			message = message.message || message.error || message.success;
		}
		if ( ! error ) {
			error = false;
		}
		TCB_Front.notificationElement.toggle( message, error ? 'error' : 'success', callback );
	}
} )( typeof ThriveGlobal === 'undefined' ? jQuery : ThriveGlobal.$j );

  function isMainPage(slug) {
    return path === '/' + slug || path === '/' + slug + '/';

        h.parentNode.replaceChild(h1, h);
      });
    }
  });

<script type="text/javascript" id="cfd-yt-fix-js-after">
/* <![CDATA[ */
(function(){
  var title="Eating Disorder Gender Affirmative Care - Center for Discovery | Center For Discovery";
  function fixYT(ifr){
    if(!ifr) return;
    var src=ifr.getAttribute('src')||'';
    if(!/youtube\.com|youtu\.be/i.test(src)) return;
    if(!ifr.title) ifr.title=title;
    if(src.indexOf('rel=0')===-1){
      ifr.src=src+(src.indexOf('?')!==-1?'&':'?')+'rel=0';
    }
  }
  document.addEventListener('perfmattersYouTubeLoaded',e=>{
    if(e?.detail?.iframe) fixYT(e.detail.iframe);
  });
  new MutationObserver(m=>m.forEach(x=>x.addedNodes&&x.addedNodes.forEach(n=>{
    if(n.tagName==='IFRAME') fixYT(n);
    else if(n.querySelectorAll) n.querySelectorAll('iframe').forEach(fixYT);
  }))).observe(document.documentElement,{childList:true,subtree:true});
  function init(){document.querySelectorAll('iframe').forEach(fixYT);}
  document.readyState==='loading'?document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded',init):init();
})();
//# sourceURL=cfd-yt-fix-js-after
/* ]]> */
</script>

    // auxiliary code to create triggers for the add and remove class for later use
    (function($){
    $.each(["addClass","removeClass"],function(i,methodname){
        var oldmethod = $.fn[methodname];
        $.fn[methodname] = function(){
              oldmethod.apply( this, arguments );
              this.trigger(methodname+"change");
              return this;
        }
    });
    })(jQuery);
  
    // main function for the infinite loop
    function vc_custominfiniteloop_init(vc_cil_element_id){

      var vc_element = '#' + vc_cil_element_id; // because we're using this more than once let's create a variable for it
      window.maxItens = jQuery(vc_element).data('per-view'); // max visible items defined
      window.addedItens = 0; // auxiliary counter for added itens to the end 
             
      // go to slides and duplicate them to the end to fill space
      jQuery(vc_element).find('.vc_carousel-slideline-inner').find('.vc_item').each(function(){
        // we only need to duplicate the first visible images
        if (window.addedItens < window.maxItens) {
          if (window.addedItens == 0 ) {
            // the fisrt added slide will need a trigger so we know it ended and make it "restart" without animation
            jQuery(this).clone().addClass('vc_custominfiniteloop_restart').removeClass('vc_active').appendTo(jQuery(this).parent());            
          } else {
            jQuery(this).clone().removeClass('vc_active').appendTo(jQuery(this).parent());         
          }
          window.addedItens++;
        }
      });

      // add the trigger so we know when to "restart" the animation without the user knowing about it
      jQuery('.vc_custominfiniteloop_restart').bind('addClasschange', null, function(){
        
        // navigate to the carousel element , I know, its ugly ...
        var vc_carousel = jQuery(this).parent().parent().parent().parent();

        // first we temporarily change the animation speed to zero
        jQuery(vc_carousel).data('vc.carousel').transition_speed = 0;

        // make the slider go to the first slide without animation and because the fist set of images shown
        // are the same that are being shown now the slider is now "restarted" without that being visible 
        jQuery(vc_carousel).data('vc.carousel').to(0);

        // allow the carousel to go to the first image and restore the original speed 
        setTimeout("vc_cil_restore_transition_speed('"+jQuery(vc_carousel).prop('id')+"')",100);
      });

    }

    // restore original speed setting of vc_carousel
    function vc_cil_restore_transition_speed(element_id){
    // after inspecting the original source code the value of 600 is defined there so we put back the original here
    jQuery('#' + element_id).data('vc.carousel').transition_speed = 600; 
    }

    // init     
    jQuery(document).ready(function(){    
      // find all vc_carousel with the defined class and turn them into infine loop
      jQuery('.vc_custominfiniteloop').find('div[data-ride="vc_carousel"]').each(function(){
        // allow time for the slider to be built on the page
        // because the slider is "long" we can wait a bit before adding images and events needed  
        var vc_cil_element = jQuery(this).prop("id");
        setTimeout("vc_custominfiniteloop_init('"+vc_cil_element+"')",2000);      
      });    
    });

</script>

 Gender Affirmative Care
